_note: Ready-to-insert interactive_image block for a flower (longitudinal section). Image: flower_illus.webp (keyed transparent + compressed from the founder's generated PNG, 1456x1090ish landscape). To ship: upload the webp to R2 via the books asset-upload route, put the returned URL in `src`, and add this block to the target biology page (Class 11 Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants). Hotspots authored + browser-verified against the image on 2026-07-15 (all placements confirmed live, including the tight anther/filament/stigma cluster with no pill-overlap, 3-combo streak). COORDINATES CORRECTED 2026-07-15 (f): prepare_illustration.py was fixed to crop transparent canvas padding before resizing (was previously only resizing, leaving unused margin around the subject) -- this image was regenerated tighter, and every hotspot fraction below was recomputed via an exact affine transform (not re-eyeballed) from the old to the new crop box. Re-verified live in the browser after the fix.
caption: 📸 Tap each dot to explore — or hit 'Test yourself' to play Label Sprint

hotspots.label: Petal
hotspots.detail: Often brightly coloured to **attract pollinators**; together the petals form the corolla.

hotspots.label: Sepal
hotspots.detail: The outermost, usually green, whorl that protects the flower bud before it opens.

hotspots.label: Anther
hotspots.detail: The pollen-producing sac at the tip of the stamen.

hotspots.label: Filament
hotspots.detail: The thin stalk that holds the anther up; together they form the **stamen**, the male part of the flower.

hotspots.label: Stigma
hotspots.detail: The sticky tip of the pistil that **receives pollen**.

hotspots.label: Style
hotspots.detail: The tube connecting the stigma to the ovary, through which the pollen tube grows.

hotspots.label: Ovary
hotspots.detail: The swollen base of the pistil containing the ovules; develops into the **fruit** after fertilisation.

hotspots.label: Ovule
hotspots.detail: Contains the egg cell; develops into a **seed** after fertilisation.

hotspots.label: Thalamus (receptacle)
hotspots.detail: The swollen tip of the flower stalk where all the floral whorls (sepals, petals, stamens, pistil) attach.

generation_prompt: A clean anatomically accurate educational botanical illustration of a typical flower cut in longitudinal section, standard biology-textbook layout, semi-realistic painterly style. Show clearly separated: sepal, petal, anther, filament, stigma, style, ovary, ovule, thalamus (receptacle). Muted palette, flat, solid deep-charcoal background #121316, no text/labels/numbers/leader lines. Landscape 4:3, high resolution.
